title: Another BFS

starterCode.python:
class Solution:
    # @param A : integer
    # @param B : list of list of integers
    # @param C : integer
    # @param D : integer
    # @return an integer
    def solve(self, A, B, C, D):

hints: Use 0-1 BFS (modified BFS) since edge weights are only 1 or 2.
Build an adjacency list from the edge list.
Use a deque: add weight-1 edges to the front (priority) and weight-2 edges to the back.
Maintain a distance array initialized to infinity, set distance[C] = 0.
If distance[D] remains infinity after BFS, return -1.
